title: Mailchimp vs Buttondown (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Mailchimp and Buttondown email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which suits your needs best.
intro: Mailchimp and Buttondown are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences with distinct philosophies. Mailchimp focuses on all-in-one marketing automation for small businesses, while Buttondown prioritizes simplicity and markdown-based writing for newsletters and technical creators. Choosing between them depends on your technical comfort level, audience size, and desired feature set.

comparison_table.tool_a: Landing pages, A/B testing, audience segmentation
comparison_table.tool_b: API access, paid subscriptions, multiple newsletters

choose_a_if: You're a beginner or small business owner seeking an all-in-one marketing platform with drag-and-drop simplicity
You need landing pages, advanced A/B testing, and audience segmentation features built-in
You want to start with 500 free subscribers and potentially scale with an affiliate program
choose_b_if: You're a developer, technical writer, or markdown enthusiast who prefers writing in plain text
You want to offer paid subscriptions to your newsletter readers directly through the platform
You need API access and value customer support highly (based on ratings)

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Mailchimp is better for beginners and small businesses requiring comprehensive marketing tools, while Buttondown excels for technical creators and newsletter writers who prefer markdown. Buttondown has significantly higher customer satisfaction ratings, but Mailchimp offers more features and a larger free tier.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Mailchimp or Buttondown?
faq.a: Buttondown is slightly cheaper at $9/month compared to Mailchimp's $13/month. However, Mailchimp's free tier supports 500 subscribers versus Buttondown's 100, which may delay when you need to upgrade depending on your audience size.

faq.q: Can I switch from Buttondown to Mailchimp?
faq.a: Yes, Buttondown offers import and export functionality, making it straightforward to migrate your subscriber lists and content to Mailchimp. Both platforms support standard email list formats, so transitioning between them is relatively painless.
